name: Wisconsin Auto Title Loans, INC.
description_short: Milwaukee storefront lender offering auto title loans up to $15,000 and signature installment loans up to $1,500 with a quick online application.
description_long:
Wisconsin Auto Title Loans, Inc. is a consumer lending storefront located at 6863-65 North 76th Street in Milwaukee, Wisconsin. The company is affiliated with CLA Corp (evidenced by the corporate email domain clacorp.com and the shared toll-free number 800-922-8803), which operates multiple title loan branches across Wisconsin. The Milwaukee branch markets itself directly to consumers facing unexpected financial shortfalls — late rent, unpaid bills, sudden expenses — and positions speed as its primary competitive advantage.

The lender offers three distinct products. Auto title loans, secured by the borrower's vehicle title, are available up to $15,000 and represent the flagship product. Online title loans follow the same structure but are processed remotely. Signature installment loans, which require no vehicle collateral, are available up to $1,500. All three products can be initiated through the company's online application, which collects personal information, contact details, vehicle year/make/model, and consent to electronic disclosure delivery. In-person processing is also available at the Milwaukee storefront.

The Milwaukee location operates six days per week — Monday through Friday, 10AM to 6PM, and Saturday from 9AM to 1PM — providing weekend access uncommon among traditional lenders. The company publishes a dedicated Rates, Fees & Terms page and a Consumer Disclosure page on its website, and requires applicants to consent to electronic delivery of all loan documents before submission. An online payment portal is also available for existing borrowers. The company's branding around 'fastest title loans Milwaukee WI has to offer' reflects a clear emphasis on speed of funding over cost competitiveness.

Consumers considering this lender should be aware of the structural risks of auto title loans. The website does not disclose APR figures in the content reviewed, but title loans from storefront lenders across the industry typically carry triple-digit APRs and short repayment windows. Borrowers who cannot repay risk losing their vehicle, since the title serves as collateral. The installment loan ceiling of $1,500 also limits usefulness for larger emergencies. This lender is most appropriate as a last resort after exhausting credit union payday alternative loans, non-profit credit counseling, and personal loan options from lower-cost sources.
pros: Title loans up to $15,000 — higher ceiling than most payday and emergency cash lenders
Online application available — borrowers can start the process without visiting the branch
Saturday hours (9AM–1PM) accommodate borrowers unable to visit on weekdays
Two collateral options — vehicle-secured title loans or unsecured installment loans
Published Consumer Disclosure and Rates, Fees & Terms pages available on website
Online payment portal for existing borrowers to manage repayments
Phone and email contact publicly listed (800-922-8803, webquestions@clacorp.com)
cons: Vehicle title is required as collateral for the primary product — default means repossession risk
APR and fee structure are not disclosed in the main website content; borrowers must navigate a separate rates page
Installment loans capped at $1,500 — insufficient for mid-range emergency needs
Sunday hours are unavailable — Saturday closes at 1PM, limiting weekend access
Branch of CLA Corp, a multi-location title lender, not an independent or community-based lender
best_for: Milwaukee residents who own a vehicle outright or with low remaining balance and need immediate cash
Consumers with damaged credit who cannot qualify for personal loans or credit union PALs
Borrowers needing amounts between $1,500 and $15,000 secured against a vehicle
Those needing a small unsecured emergency loan up to $1,500 without vehicle collateral
diagnosis: Wisconsin Auto Title Loans, Inc. is best suited for Milwaukee-area borrowers with vehicle equity who need fast cash and have exhausted lower-cost options. The core caveat is the collateral risk: defaulting on a title loan means potential vehicle repossession, making this an expensive and high-stakes product that should only be used when alternatives like credit union PALs, employer advances, or personal loans are genuinely unavailable.
services: In-person loan processing at Milwaukee storefront
Online payment portal for existing borrowers
Published rates, fees, and terms disclosure
